title: MachineQuery Object
description: MachineQuery defines the query to access Machines.

methods.name: classType
methods.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().

methods.name: execute
methods.description: Executes the query for specific machines based on the query's properties.

properties.name: isValid
properties.description: Indicates if this object is still valid, i.e. hasn't been deleted or some other action done to invalidate the reference.

properties.name: location
properties.description: The location specifies the location to search in the machine library. Setting the location clears any previous specified URL.

properties.name: model
properties.description: The case-insensitive model specifies the model of the machine. The default empty model applies to all machines.

properties.name: objectType
properties.description: This property is supported by all objects in the API and returns a string that contains the full name (namespace::objecttype) describing the type of the object.   It's often useful to use this in combination with the classType method to see if an object is a certain type. For example: if obj.objectType == adsk.core.Point3D.classType():

properties.name: url
properties.description: The URL specifies the location and folder to search for in the machine library. Setting the URL updates the location.

properties.name: vendor
properties.description: The case-insensitive vendor specifies the vendor of the machine. The default empty vendor applies to all machines.

classType.title: MachineQuery.classType Method
classType.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().
classType.return_description: Returns a string indicating the type of the object.

execute.title: MachineQuery.execute Method
execute.description: Executes the query for specific machines based on the query's properties.
execute.return_description: Returns a list of `Machine`. Each returned machine matches this query.

isValid.title: MachineQuery.isValid Property
isValid.description: Indicates if this object is still valid, i.e. hasn't been deleted or some other action done to invalidate the reference.

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = machineQuery_var.isValid

location.title: MachineQuery.location Property
location.description: The location specifies the location to search in the machine library. Setting the location clears any previous specified URL.

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = machineQuery_var.location

# Set the value of the property.
machineQuery_var.location = propertyValue

model.title: MachineQuery.model Property
model.description: The case-insensitive model specifies the model of the machine. The default empty model applies to all machines.

syntax.python:
